Showcase

Testable runtime workbench

A focused example page for overlays, menus, tabs, and operator feedback controls.

OpsRuntime workbench

3 healthy

Operational queue

The workbench keeps the primary data view and the action affordances close together.

Release queue

The table and pagination make the workbench easy to exercise in both golden and browser tests.

search-apiCanaryHealthy
billingQueuedWaiting

Interactive controls

These widgets are the browser-test targets for this issue.

Approve rollout

The dialog stays testable because all behavior is attached through data attributes on server-rendered markup.

Confirm the canary has remained healthy for five minutes before promoting the release.

Operator notes

The sheet is useful for secondary details that should not crowd the main grid.

Escalation path

Use the paging rotation if the canary error budget drops below target.

  • Page platform on-call
  • Pause the rollout
  • Post an update in Slack
View service logs

Incident note saved

The toast is a lightweight acknowledgement for non-blocking actions.

The overview panel highlights the steady-state health of the rollout.

Keyboard arrow keys move focus between tabs without a client-side framework.

The handoff panel keeps operator notes server-rendered and easy to snapshot test.
